name: CONTRACT-BACKLOG-21
title: Federate build manifests: contract owns publish schema, each feature owns publish.json
description:
Currently contract/build-contract.json is a monolithic list of every copy-file target across all features. This creates cross-scope coupling: adding a script to any feature requires editing contract — a bounded-scope violation.

Proposed federated model:
1. contract owns publish-manifest.schema.json — the JSON schema every feature publish manifest must conform to. build-contract.json is retired or repurposed as schema-only.
2. Each feature owns its own publish.json declaring its copy-file targets (skills, hooks, commands, scripts) to the deployed surface under .claude/.
3. rabbit-cage/build.py becomes the discovery-and-execute runner: finds all feature publish.json files, validates each against contract schema, and executes the copies.
4. rabbit-cage/sync-check.py Stop hook updates to iterate all feature publish.json files for drift detection instead of reading the single central manifest.

Benefits: adding a new script or skill to any feature requires editing only that feature's publish.json — zero contract touch. Drift check coverage becomes more complete since each feature's manifest is authoritative for its own surface. Root cause of rabbit-config scripts/ missing from deployed surface (only SKILL.md is declared in build-contract.json, scripts/ was never added because that would require a contract touch).

history.note: Temporarily closed before CONTRACT-BACKLOG-19 housekeeping wave to prevent accidental closure by wave b/b filings; will be reopened after the wave completes
Implemented via PR #184: per-feature publish.json, build.py federated discovery, sync-check.py updated, build-contract.json retired. rabbit-config.py script gap fixed.
